content:
VERDICT: ADOPT-A
REASONS: The least-bad fix is to acknowledge the distinct category these pages are actually using: they are not tagging a sense of meaning, but the epistemology of measuring meaning. That is coherent as a controlled extension if it is named transparently, because it preserves the information without pretending it belongs to the existing senses vocabulary. ADOPT-B would smuggle in two near-synonyms and muddy the list; ADOPT-C would silently discard a real distinction just because the linter currently misses base/ pages.
CONDITIONS: 1. Make clear in the vocabulary page that `measurement-epistemic` is an out-of-scope-but-allowed adjunct category, not a sense-of-meaning tag. 2. Leave the sub-tag-control issue for a separate decision, since it is orthogonal to this ratification.
